channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: MP Nyoro Urges Responsible Leadership and Condemns Intimidation Tactics
item.description: Embu MP Ndindi Nyoro has been speaking out against what he calls irresponsible leadership and the misuse of state power in Kenya. He’s urging leaders to be more mindful of their language and actions, ...

item.title: Exploring Agnes Hathaway and the Rise of Green Witchcraft
item.description: The 2026 Oscars celebrated Jessie Buckley for her portrayal of a grieving mother in 'Hamnet', but the story also highlights Agnes Hathaway, a 'green witch'. In Maggie O'Farrell's novel, Agnes is depic...

item.title: 18 Notable Examples of Biophilic Architecture Reshaping Our Cities
item.description: Biophilic architecture, which blends natural elements into urban environments, is rapidly becoming a mainstream necessity rather than a mere environmental gesture. This design philosophy highlights th...

item.title: Troops Eliminate 74 Terrorists, Key Commanders, and Disrupt Oil Theft Activities
item.description: The Armed Forces of Nigeria made significant progress in the fight against insecurity, as they neutralized 74 terrorists, including top commanders and bomb experts, during operations across the countr...

item.title: Sixth Indian Dies in West Asia Conflict; Uttarakhand High Court Upholds FIR Against Gym Owner Deepak Kumar
item.description: An Indian citizen has passed away in Saudi Arabia amidst ongoing tensions in West Asia, bringing the total number of Indian fatalities in the conflict to six. The Directorate General of Shipping repor...

item.title: Super Micro Co-Founder Accused of Illegally Exporting AI Servers With Nvidia Chips to China
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has charged Yih-Shyan 'Wally' Liaw, co-founder of Super Micro Computer, along with Ruei-Tsang 'Steven' Chang and Ting-Wei 'Willy' Sun, for illegally exporting servers co...

item.title: James Brahe: Teenage AFL umpire and mountain biker overcoming juvenile arthritis
item.description: Seventeen-year-old James Brahe from Tuggeranong has shown remarkable resilience in the face of chronic juvenile arthritis, a severe autoimmune condition he has lived with since 16 months old. Despite ...

item.title: Rajya Sabha election in Madhya Pradesh is extremely close with Congress facing tight contest
item.description: The Rajya Sabha election in Madhya Pradesh has turned into a nail-biting contest with just five votes making the difference, putting the Congress party under significant pressure. In the latest round ...

item.title: MI5 Contractor Found Not Guilty by Reason of Insanity After Disclosing Classified Information
item.description: An MI5 contractor, Juan Joseph, has been found not guilty by reason of insanity after disclosing classified intelligence information to a foreign power. The 43-year-old IT specialist had worked for MI...

item.title: Investigative lapses and missing evidence compromise justice in Kilifi triple murder case
item.description: The Kilifi triple murder case, which shook the coastal community in July 2021, has ended with more frustration than justice. Land agent James Kafani Mzungu, businessman Sidik Anverali Sumra, and drive...

item.title: Spring Arrives, But Canada’s Weather Will Warm Slowly Over the Coming Weeks
item.description: As the official start of spring arrives, Canadians are experiencing weather conditions far from the typical sunny skies. Snow, rain, fog, and ice crystals are still present across the country. Accordi...

item.title: Action legend Chuck Norris passes away peacefully at 86
item.description: Legendary action star and martial artist Chuck Norris has passed away at the age of 86, his family confirmed in a heartfelt social media post. In their message, they expressed their deep sorrow over h...

item.title: William Kamket's Children Pay Tribute at Late Brother's Memorial
item.description: William Kamket's family recently went through a very difficult time following the death of their young son, Bill Ballot Kassait Jr., who passed away while receiving treatment in hospital. The memorial...

item.title: Two arrested in CDO drug bust, P20 million worth of shabu seized
item.description: Anti-narcotics operatives in Cagayan de Oro City successfully arrested two drug suspects and seized an estimated P20.4 million worth of suspected shabu in a sting operation conducted along Coastal Roa...

item.title: Reclaiming Human Dignity in Everyday South African Life
item.description: In her reflective piece for Human Rights Day, Akhona Ngcobo explores what she calls South Africa’s 'dignity deficit'—a subtle, ongoing erosion of respect and recognition that affects ordinary interact...

item.title: BBC Broadcaster Dame Jenni Murray Dies Aged 75 After Decades of Influential Work on Woman's Hour
item.description: Dame Jenni Murray, a highly respected broadcaster and journalist best known for her long-standing role as presenter of BBC Radio 4’s Woman’s Hour, has died at the age of 75. Murray, who first joined t...

item.title: PSSSF Supports Vulnerable Communities in Zanzibar with Iftar and Grants
item.description: The Public Service Social Security Fund (PSSSF) has demonstrated its strong commitment to social welfare in Zanzibar by hosting a special Iftar event and providing vital support to vulnerable groups. ...

item.title: Cederberg Municipality Faces Challenges with Growing Informal Settlements in Clanwilliam and Citrusdal
item.description: The Cederberg Local Municipality is struggling to provide adequate services to its rapidly growing informal settlements in Clanwilliam and Citrusdal. Almost one in five residents live in shacks and ma...

item.title: Middle East conflict escalates with attacks on Kuwait refinery and assassination of Iranian official
item.description: This week, the conflict between Iran, Israel, and the U.S. intensified, affecting global energy and security. Iran launched an attack on the Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery in Kuwait using drones, causing...
